meta.title: Dynamic STEM Innovation Potential Identification System for K-12 Students
meta.description: Powered by Complex Adaptive Systems theory and AI multimodal behavioral analysis, dynamically identifying STEM innovation potential in students from grade 4 to grade 12 — replacing one-off pen-and-paper testing.

footer.partners: In partnership with: Shanghai Jiao Tong University × the Shanghai Institute of AI Education, East China Normal University
footer.copyright: © {year} STEM Potential Tracking System. All rights reserved.

hero.badge: Grounded in Complex Adaptive Systems Theory · Aligned with National Science Education Policy
hero.subtitle: Not a one-time label, but a continuously tracked growth portrait

comparison.eyebrow: Why STEM potential identification needs to evolve
comparison.title: Traditional Assessment vs. Our Approach
comparison.description: The same child looks completely different under these two approaches.

traditionalItems.title: One-off pen-and-paper testing
traditionalItems.description: A single exam decides the verdict, with no view of the real exploration process

traditionalItems.title: Generic ability measurement
traditionalItems.description: Detached from authentic tasks, it misses what makes STEM potential distinctive

traditionalItems.title: Outcome-based labeling
traditionalItems.description: Only the final score counts — a label, not a growth story

traditionalItems.title: A single grade-level snapshot
traditionalItems.description: No continuous tracking across school stages, so trends stay invisible

traditionalItems.title: Cognitive dimension only
traditionalItems.description: Overlooks creativity, motivation, personality, and other key factors

breakthroughItems.title: Continuous, full-process behavioral tracking
breakthroughItems.description: Captures the full trail of iteration, trial-and-error, and reflection

breakthroughItems.title: Embedded in authentic STEM task scenarios
breakthroughItems.description: Emerges naturally within inquiry, engineering, and technology-innovation tasks

breakthroughItems.title: A visible, evolving growth portrait
breakthroughItems.description: Continuously updated, never a one-time label

breakthroughItems.title: Continuous coverage from Grade 4 to Grade 12
breakthroughItems.description: Nine grades tracked end-to-end, so growth trajectories become visible

breakthroughItems.title: Six dimensions: cognition, creativity, motivation, personality, emotion, and engineering practice
breakthroughItems.description: A full picture across six facets of STEM potential

techRoute.title: A complete loop from task scenario to dynamic portrait
techRoute.description: The system is built on the following five core stages, safeguarded throughout by a dedicated data security and ethics governance layer.

nodes.title: Scenario Design & Digital Modeling
nodes.detail: Following the laws of innovative talent development, the system uses complex real-world problems — drawn from national science and technology priorities and social needs — as task carriers to create highly open-ended, challenging, and iterative STEM task scenarios: spanning inquiry-based, engineering-design, and technology-innovation types, and progressing from intuitive-creative (elementary) to systematic-inquiry (middle school) to innovative-integrative (high school).

nodes.title: Full-Process, Multimodal Data Tracking
nodes.short: Fine-grained capture of the entire exploration path, catching non-consensus problem-solving
nodes.detail: Focuses on capturing unconventional, non-consensus, cross-disciplinary, and extreme-persistence problem-solving behavior — such as creative problem reframing, far-reaching cross-domain association, extreme focus, and unusually high iterative patience. Captures three types of data: process data (operation logs, solution iterations, reflective reviews), self-report data (STEM motivation, use of cognitive and metacognitive strategies), and observable data (engagement, task completion, and final work quality).

nodes.title: Longitudinal Cross-Stage Tracking Mechanism
nodes.short: Grade-appropriate tasks that track potential development trajectories over time
nodes.detail: Periodic, grade-appropriate STEM tasks are administered across school stages to track students' potential development trajectories and trends over time.

nodes.title: Human-AI Collaborative Data Analysis
nodes.short: AI pattern recognition + expert qualitative judgment
nodes.detail: Grounded in machine learning theory and multimodal behavioral analysis, led by STEM education experts and teachers, to uncover the mapping between behavioral data and STEM potential.

nodes.title: Dynamic Portrait Generation
nodes.short: A continuously updated growth portrait, not a one-time label
nodes.detail: Generates a dynamic STEM potential portrait covering strengths, development level, growth rate, and trends — shared with teachers, school administrators, parents, and education authorities.

techRoute.governanceNote: Throughout: data security & ethics governance (see dedicated section below)
techRoute.expandLabel: View the full description for each stage
techRoute.academicSupport: This technical approach is academically supported by faculty teams at Shanghai Jiao Tong University and East China Normal University [wording pending final review by the team]

features.title: Five Feature Modules
features.description: Mapped to the five core stages of the technical approach, simplified for teachers, school administrators, and parents.

items.title: Authentic STEM Task Scenarios
items.description: Open-ended, challenging, iterative inquiry, engineering-design, and technology-innovation tasks that reveal potential through real problems, not pen-and-paper questions.

items.title: Full-Process Multimodal Behavior Tracking
items.description: Looks beyond the outcome to record the complete trail of iteration, trial-and-error, and reflection, capturing unconventional and non-consensus problem-solving.

items.title: Six-Dimension Indicator System
items.description: Engineering practice, scientific cognition, creative thinking, motivation and self-efficacy, personality and emotional regulation — simplified, not an academic indicator dump.

items.title: Human-AI Collaborative Analysis
items.description: AI pattern recognition handles data at scale, while STEM experts and teachers provide qualitative judgment — together revealing signals of potential.

items.title: Dynamic Growth Portrait
items.description: Continuously updated strengths, development level, growth rate, and trends — not a one-time assessment report with a fixed label.

cases.title: Seeing Potential in Authentic Tasks
cases.description: Take the "Rocket Balance" rocket-design simulation task as an example: students repeatedly tune parameters and iterate under real constraints.
cases.narrativeText: Students must design and simulate a rocket flight plan under limited fuel, payload, and structural constraints. The system logs every parameter adjustment, every simulation result, and every iteration — not just whether the final launch succeeds.
cases.statsNote: // TODO: replace with real data; data source pending confirmation from the project team
cases.partnersNote: Partner school logos pending authorization from the project team

security.title: Secure, Trustworthy, Compliant
security.description: A governance layer that runs throughout the system, ensuring every piece of behavioral data is handled with care.

items.title: Data Anonymization
items.description: Student behavioral data is anonymized; analysis and reporting do not directly expose identity information.

items.title: Encrypted Storage
items.description: Data is encrypted end-to-end, in storage and in transit, reducing the risk of leaks or tampering.

items.title: Tiered Authorization
items.description: Teachers, school administrators, parents, and education authorities access data by role, on a least-privilege basis.

items.title: Informed Consent
items.description: An informed-consent process with schools and families is completed before assessment begins.

items.title: Ethics Review
items.description: An ethics review process ensures task design and data use comply with educational ethics standards.

technicalPage.heroDescription: The system's technical approach is designed around the principles of innovative-talent development, comprising five core stages safeguarded throughout by a dedicated data security and ethics governance layer. Presented here in full, in line with the official technical approach documentation.
taskChain: Present the task goal and an open-ended problem scenario; students independently define, pose, and analyze the problem
Introduce real-world constraints — resources, cost, ethics; students propose creative solutions
Students simulate and test their solution in the system, then iterate

scenario: Inquiry-based, engineering-design, and technology-innovation task types
Integrated design from elementary through high school
Task chain: define the problem → real-world constraints → simulate and iterate
tracking: Process data: operation logs, solution iterations, reflective reviews
Self-report data: STEM motivation, use of cognitive and metacognitive strategies
Observable data: engagement, task completion, and work quality
longitudinal: Periodic, grade-appropriate STEM tasks across school stages
Dynamically tracks students' potential development trajectories and trends
analysis: Multimodal behavioral analysis grounded in machine learning theory
STEM experts and teachers jointly lead qualitative judgment
Uncovers the mapping between behavioral data and STEM potential
portrait: Strengths, development level, growth rate, and trends
Shared with teachers, school administrators, parents, and education authorities
Continuously updated, not a one-time report

technicalPage.governanceIntro: The following governance measures run throughout all five core stages, ensuring the system is secure, trustworthy, and compliant. See the full "Data Security & Ethics" section on the homepage.

theories.name: PISA
theories.description: An OECD-led international assessment of student reading, math, and science literacy

theories.name: NAEP
theories.description: The U.S. "National Assessment of Educational Progress," a long-standing authoritative measure of student achievement

theories.name: OECD SSES
theories.description: The OECD Survey on Social and Emotional Skills, focused on students' social and emotional development

theories.name: Zimmerman's SRL Model
theories.description: Zimmerman's Self-Regulated Learning model, emphasizing goal-setting, strategy use, and self-reflection

technicalPage.theoryDisclaimer: Wording and authorization for these theoretical citations are pending final confirmation from the project team [TBD]

productsPage.heroDescription: For schools, regional education bureaus, and provincial education departments — a STEM potential tracking system powered by Complex Adaptive Systems theory and AI multimodal behavioral analysis, replacing one-off pen-and-paper testing.

audiences.title: Schools
audiences.description: For early pilots and single-school validation, paired with a teacher dashboard.

audiences.title: Regional Education Bureaus
audiences.description: Cross-school and regional comparative analysis to support education policy decisions.

audiences.title: Provincial Education Departments
audiences.description: Large-scale, customized deployment supporting policy evaluation and resource allocation.

productsPage.dimensionsIntro: The STEM Potential Behavioral Indicator System includes 6 primary dimensions and 22 secondary indicators, covering 9 grade levels (Grade 4 to Grade 12), backed by 15 core references. Below are the five primary dimensions officially named so far:

dimensions.title: Engineering Practice & Technology Innovation
dimensions.description: The full engineering practice cycle, from problem identification to solution design, prototyping, and iterative testing.

dimensions.title: Scientific Cognition & Reasoning
dimensions.description: Understanding of scientific concepts, hypothesis testing, data analysis, and systems thinking.

dimensions.title: STEM Creative Thinking
dimensions.description: Divergent and convergent thinking, plus scientific problem discovery.

dimensions.title: Motivation, Self-Efficacy & Self-Regulated Learning
dimensions.description: Intrinsic motivation, self-efficacy, and self-regulated learning strategies.

productsPage.dimensionsDisclaimer: The indicator system document lists five named primary-dimension groups in its body text, while the summary figure cites "6 primary dimensions"; the exact breakdown is pending final confirmation from the project team [TBD]
productsPage.whitepaperDescription: For education bureau and school decision-makers, covering the complete 22 secondary indicators and grade-level weighting.
productsPage.whitepaperNote: // TODO: replace with a real download link; whether registration is required is pending confirmation from the project team [TBD]
productsPage.ctaDescription: See partnership plans for schools, regional education bureaus, and provincial education departments, or book a product demo directly.

casesPage.heroDescription: Take the "Rocket Balance" rocket-design simulation task as an example, showing the complete process of students tuning parameters and iterating under real constraints.
casesPage.narrativeText: Students must design and simulate a rocket flight plan under limited fuel, payload, and structural constraints. The system logs every parameter adjustment, every simulation result, and every iteration — not just whether the final launch succeeds.

processSteps.title: Present the Task & Constraints
processSteps.description: Present the rocket flight task goal and an open-ended design problem; students define the key variables

processSteps.title: Set Real-World Constraints
processSteps.description: Introduce fuel, payload, and structural constraints; students propose creative solutions

processSteps.title: Simulate & Iterate
processSteps.description: Students simulate the flight plan in the system and repeatedly tune parameters
